reasoning: The market estimate on 2025-08-21 was 24%. 'Inflation rate in 2025' typically refers to the December 2025 YoY print or the annual average. Recent data shows headline US CPI was 2.7% for the 12 months ending in July 2025, with core inflation at 3.1%. The Cleveland Fed Nowcast projects August CPI around 2.86%. While headline CPI dipped to 2.3% in April 2025 due to energy prices, forecasts for the second half of 2025 broadly predict a re-acceleration. JPMorgan projects 3.5% by Q4, RBC expects a 'squeeze higher', and Goldman Sachs forecasts ~3% due to the impact of Trump's tariffs and other policies taking effect. Only Morgan Stanley forecasts 2.5%, which would barely miss resolving 'below 2.5%'. Given that the July 2.7% print and these forecasts were already public when the market was at 24%, I am slightly discounting the market's probability due to the very consistent consensus that tariffs and shelter costs will maintain inflation above the 2.5% target through the end of 2025. An unexpected recession or sharp drop in energy prices is the main path to a YES resolution, meriting a ~19% probability.
